merged_events.event_description: The American Legation in St. Petersburg was established when U.S. Minister John Quincy Adams presented his credentials to Emperor Alexander I.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in St. Petersburg was established on November 5, 1809, when U.S. Minister to Russia John Quincy Adams presented his credentials to Emperor Alexander I.

merged_events.event_description: The American Legation in St. Petersburg was raised to an Embassy when Ethan A. Hitchcock was appointed Ambassador to Russia.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in St. Petersburg was raised to an Embassy on February 11, 1898, when Ethan A. Hitchcock was appointed Ambassador to Russia.

merged_events.event_description: The American Embassy at Archangel was closed, ending formal U.S. diplomatic presence in Russia.

evidence.quote: The American Embassy at Archangel was closed on September 14, 1919.

merged_events.event_description: The American Embassy was established in Moscow when William Bullitt presented his credentials to President Kalinin as U.S. Ambassador to the Soviet Union, resuming formal diplomatic representation after a 14-year break.

evidence.quote: On December 13, 1933, William Bullitt presented his credentials to President Kalinin in Moscow as the U.S. Ambassador to the Soviet Union.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Legation in Russia elevated to Embassy when Ethan Allen Hitchcock was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: Ethan Allen Hitchcock, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1897-08-16. Credentials presented: 1897-12-16. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1898-03-21.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1809-11-05) and status (Legation). RDCR confirms Adams presented credentials on this date.

CSV date 1898-02-11 matches RDCR (Event 1) institutional date exactly. POCOM (Event 4) gives 1898-03-21 as Hitchcock's promotion date, which is a separate personnel event. Per source priority, RDCR wins with the institutional elevation date.
Exact match on date (1919-09-14) and status (None). RDCR confirms closure of the American Embassy at Archangel.
Exact match on date (1933-12-13) and status (Embassy). RDCR confirms Bullitt presented credentials to President Kalinin in Moscow.

unsupported_in_sources.notes: No extracted event corresponds to a 'Liaison' status on 1917-11-07. This date corresponds to the Bolshevik Revolution (October Revolution), which disrupted U.S.-Russian diplomatic relations, but neither the RDCR nor POCOM extractions mention a formal transition to 'Liaison' status on this date. The RDCR narrative jumps from the Embassy period to the closure at Archangel in 1919. This event may be based on historical inference or sources outside this pipeline.
